findings.message: AC-19 uses `BranchMode::Create / resume flow` as the negated condition, but the rest of the track defines the relaxed preflight as `matches!(mode, BranchMode::Auto) && already_materialized && resume_allowed` and the verification text separately checks `BranchMode::Auto && already_materialized=false`. This mixes an unrelated `Create` mode into the acceptance criterion and conflicts with T012/AC-20.

T005 only adds `Bash` for `type-designer.md`, but `IN-09` and `AC-17` require `Write + Bash` for all three subagents and explicitly require `type-designer` to directly write `<layer>-types.json`. As written, this task cannot satisfy the acceptance criteria for subagent write permission.

`CN-10` says revised `track/*.md` bodies should contain only writer invocation and result receive, but `T007`/`AC-14` require a `TaskCreate` preamble in `/track:plan`. That preamble is extra command-body logic, so the plan contradicts its own command-slimming rule unless `CN-10` is relaxed first.

`AC-19` names `BranchMode::Create` as the false-path test condition, but `IN-10` and `T012` define the allowed true path as `BranchMode::Auto && already_materialized && resume_allowed`. The acceptance criterion is pointing at the wrong branch mode, so the required unit test is mis-specified.

CN-02 says each subagent may write only its corresponding SSoT file and touch nothing else, but the same spec requires those subagents to render generated view files: spec-designer renders spec.md, type-designer renders graph/contract-map/type-signal markdown, and impl-planner may render plan.md. As written, an implementation cannot satisfy both the writer-boundary constraint and the required subagent render pipeline; clarify that generated views are within the owning subagent's write scope or move rendering to another writer.

AC-13 includes init.md in the set of command definitions that must contain no schema validation/codec-round-trip procedure, but AC-01/CN-10/T001 explicitly require /track:init to perform identity schema binary verification via sotp verify track-metadata. This makes the /track:init acceptance criteria self-contradictory; AC-13 needs to exclude the /track:init identity check or AC-01/T001 need to move that validation out of the command definition.

spec.json reports `yellow: 0` even though many spec elements carry `informal_grounds`. The referenced ADR gate semantics make non-empty informal grounds yellow until they are formalized, so this artifact is claiming all 59 signals are blue while simultaneously relying on unresolved user_directive/memory grounds. That can hide the required ADR/convention formalization before merge.

CN-12 says each subagent writes only its corresponding SSoT file and nothing else, but CN-02/CN-13 and the in-scope requirements require those same subagents to render derived files such as `spec.md`, graph/type-signal views, `contract-map.md`, and `plan.md`. These are mutually exclusive write-boundary instructions for the agent definitions, so implementers cannot satisfy both constraints as written.

AC-13 explicitly applies to `.claude/commands/track/plan.md`, but task-coverage maps AC-13 only to T001-T004. The plan rewrite is done by T007/T008, so the coverage artifact can pass AC-13 without covering the command file that AC-13 names, leaving the forbidden-logic check for `plan.md` untracked.

The artifacts refer to OQ-6/OQ-7/OQ-8/OQ-10 as recorded open questions, and spec entries say differences are documented in those OQs, but the reviewed plan artifacts contain no open-question definitions or section for those labels. The OQ references are therefore not usable design records for reviewers or implementers.

AC-13 now says `/track:init` is explicitly excluded from the no-schema-validation/no-logic inspection because T001 is allowed to run `sotp verify track-metadata`, but the same rendered AC still maps AC-13 to `T001`. That leaves the plan internally inconsistent about whether `/track:init` must satisfy AC-13, and can make reviewers or implementers reject the required identity validation step.

CN-08 says every standalone phase command, including `/track:type-design`, must own its upstream artifact pre-check, but the same sentence only defines checks for `/track:spec-design` and `/track:impl-plan`; T004 and the T004 verification steps also omit any `spec.json` pre-check for `/track:type-design`. Since IN-06 marks `/track:type-design` as independently invokable, the plan leaves Phase 2 able to run without its required Phase 1 artifact despite the stated command contract.

AC-13 was revised to explicitly exclude `/track:init` because that command is the CN-10 writer=main exception, but the plan still presents T001 as satisfying AC-13: `impl-plan.json`/rendered `plan.md` list `AC-13` in T001, while `task-coverage.json` removes T001 from AC-13. That leaves the artifacts internally contradictory about whether `/track:init` must be checked under AC-13, so an implementer could apply the logic-free command-body constraint to the one command the spec now exempts.

AC-13 was changed to explicitly exclude /track:init because T001 is allowed to include identity schema validation, but the T001 verification checklist still says /track:init should be checked against AC-13. That makes the verification procedure contradict the updated acceptance criterion and can cause reviewers to apply the no-schema-logic gate to the one command the spec says is exempt.

task-coverage now claims AC-13 is covered by T008, but T008's task description does not list AC-13 among its covered criteria. Since AC-13 is the low-level-logic exclusion gate for plan.md and T008 adds the loop body to plan.md, the plan artifacts are inconsistent about whether T008 must verify that gate.

AC-13 is now assigned to T007/T008 for the rewritten `.claude/commands/track/plan.md` in the spec/task coverage, but the verification steps for T007/T008 only check the plan skeleton and loop behavior and never verify AC-13's forbidden schema/signal/reference/coverage/canonical-logic condition for `plan.md`. As written, the verification artifact no longer covers all tasks/ACs it claims to cover.

The new Open Questions section says it defines OQ labels that appear in the spec's `informal_grounds`, and it includes OQ-10, but OQ-10 is only defined in `verification.md` and is not recorded in `spec.json`/`spec.md` informal grounds. That makes OQ-10 invisible to the spec SSoT despite the verification artifact claiming it is part of the spec's informal grounds.
